ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Jan-24-2022 Continued To Apr-25-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Christina Tanti, via ZOOM, for Roy Puccinelli, Personal Representative; Azita Rahim, via ZOOM, Douglas Jones, Donna Fauber, Gail Jones, Jacqueline Winkler, Jeanine Wilson, and William Jones, Heirs.

The court notes that the only document filed in this matter since the previous hearing four months ago is an incomplete Inventory and Appraisal. The court confirms with Ms. Tanti that the home has been sold and that the proceeds are in an insured account and that an accounting is being currently being drafted. The court notes that although no petition has been filed, this matter seems to be moving forward and that the matter will be continued. The court gently reminds Ms. Tanti that a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on will need to be filed by the next calling of this matter.

The Status on Close of Estate is continued to Apr-25-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: C. Gomez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Sep-20-2021 Continued To Jan-24-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nathan Pastor appeared by zoom for Roy Puccinelli.
Evan Winet appeared by zoom for 5 other heirs.

Mr. Pastor stated that the Probate Referee has completed the inventory and appraisal, and that it was mailed to his client last week for signature. Mr. Pastor also stated that the house will be listed, that it took some time to do the repairs and get the house ready to list and sell by the end of the year, and requested a 90 to 120-day continuance. Status on close of estate is continued to January 24, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A New Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Apr-19-2021 Off Calendar
Ex Parte Application For Order Advancing Hearing

Petition To Determine Heirship
Off Calendar Mar-22-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nathan Pastor appeared by zoom for Roy Puccinelli (Administrator).
Adam Lazar appeared by zoom for Donna Fauber, Douglas Jones, Gail Jones, Jacqueline Winkler, Jeanine Wilson and William Jones (Heirs).

The Court raised the issue of whether the time period regarding Probate Code Section 11700 is jurisdictional since the petition to determine heirship was filed before letters were issued. Mr. Pastor stated that his analysis is that it is not jurisdictional, that there is no requirement in place pertaining to a timeline to file and that given it is before the Court now and his Client is the Administrator, it should be good enough. Mr. Pastor also stated that it is directional, not mandatory or jurisdictional, and that it is an issue of form over substance. Mr. Lazar stated that he agrees that there is no procedural defect in the Court entertaining the petition at this time. The Court informed Counsel that it has a different view. The Petition to Determine Heirship is off calendar. Status on filing of a new petition to determine heirship is set for April 19, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Once the petition is filed and gets a hearing date, Mr. Pastor is to file an ex parte petition to have the hearing date advanced to April 19, 2021.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Off Calendar Jan-25-2021 Continued To Mar-01-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of A Petition To Determine Persons Entitled To Distribution
Off Calendar Sep-22-2020 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Aug-05-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nathan Pastor and Christina Tanti appeared by zoom for Roy Puccinelli (Petitioner).
Jodi Phillips appeared by zoom for and with Victoria (present in Court).
Michael Covel also present in Court.

Ms. Phillips stated that she is not here to object, that she just wanted to ensure that the Petitioner post bond in the full amount and noted that the all of the heirs were noticed. The Court questions why there is no income for the $60,000.00 in personal property. Mr. Pastor stated that he believes they have not gotten access to any of the assets because no Administrator has been appointed, and that when the body was found, they found some statements and that is what their estimate is based on. The Court made sure that Mr. Pastor is aware that he needs to file a petition to determine persons entitled to distribution prior to final distribution. Mr. Pastor stated that he can get said petition on file within the next four weeks. The Court granted the Petition for Letters of Administration with full authority (subject to review of the order). Bond is set at $1,200,000.00. Status on close of estate is set for August 23, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on filing of a petition to determine persons entitled to distribution is set for September 22, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If said petition is filed by September 15, 2020, no appearance will be required.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.
